violations: references: unknown-type InteropKind::ClrInterop (kernel enum, false positive)
references: unknown-type CodeAccessPermission::revertAssert (kernel class, false positive)
references: warning unknown-type StringBuilder (self-declared false positive)

root_cause_hypothesis: Case itself PASSES. Two harness defects observed, neither case-fatal: (1) KNOWN - build_d365fo_project never runs LabelC.exe, so the newly created @Contoso:DemoInteropCsvClrError produced BPErrorUnknownLabel plus a cascading BPUnusedStrFmtArgument; running LabelC.exe manually made xppbp report 0 warnings / 0 errors, proving both warnings were harness artefacts. (2) NEW - validate_code(mode="references") raises hard ERRORS for kernel-only types absent from the AOT symbol index (InteropKind, InteropPermission, CodeAccessPermission static call), while it correctly downgrades the same situation to a warning for a declared CLR type (StringBuilder). Severity should be a warning for any unresolved identifier that matches the known kernel/system surface.
suggested_fix_area: src/tools/validate-code (reference resolver kernel-type allowlist / severity downgrade); src/tools/build (invoke LabelC.exe for models with an AxLabelFile before xppbp)
